title_en: Scholarly Reception and Use in Islamic Education
title_id: Penerimaan Ilmiah dan Penggunaan dalam Pendidikan Islam
content_id:
Al-Kaba'ir karya adz-Dzahabi telah menjadi salah satu teks etika Islam yang paling konsisten digunakan sejak penulisannya, dihargai di keempat mazhab Sunni utama karena kombinasi antara keaslian hadis dan fokus etika praktisnya. Adz-Dzahabi sendiri adalah seorang Syafi'i dalam fikih, tetapi statusnya sebagai ahli hadis berarti bahwa ulama dari semua mazhab menghormati penilaian-penilaian berbasis hadisnya.

Penerimaan karya ini sangat kuat dalam lingkungan pendidikan di mana tujuannya adalah pembentukan Islam yang praktis daripada analisis ilmiah tingkat lanjut. Kesederhanaannya membuatnya dapat ditangani oleh khalayak umum; landasan hadisnya memberikan kredibilitas ilmiah; dan fokusnya pada pelanggaran-pelanggaran yang paling serius memenuhi kebutuhan praktis yang hanya dibahas oleh karya-karya etika yang lebih komprehensif sebagai bagian dari sistem yang lebih besar. Bagi seorang penceramah yang menyampaikan ceramah tentang menghindari dosa, atau seorang guru yang memperkenalkan para pelajar pada konsep pelanggaran besar dalam hukum dan etika Islam, Al-Kaba'ir menyediakan referensi yang paling langsung dan berwibawa.

Beberapa ulama lain menulis karya-karya dalam genre yang sama, termasuk Az-Zawajir an Iqtiraf al-Kaba'ir (Penjera dari Melakukan Dosa-Dosa Besar) oleh Ibn Hajar al-Haytami al-Makki, yang lebih luas dan lebih rinci secara analitis daripada karya adz-Dzahabi. Perbandingan antara keduanya sangat instruktif: kesederhanaan adz-Dzahabi membuatnya lebih dapat diakses dan lebih langsung secara emosional, sementara komprehensivitas Ibn Hajar membuatnya lebih berguna sebagai referensi hukum dan ilmiah. Keduanya telah banyak digunakan dan terus dikonsultasikan.

Di dunia Muslim kontemporer, Al-Kaba'ir telah diterbitkan dalam berbagai edisi dengan berbagai tingkat anotasi dan tersedia dalam terjemahan dalam berbagai bahasa. Buku ini umumnya digunakan di sekolah-sekolah Islam akhir pekan, halaqah, dan program pendidikan online sebagai pengantar pemahaman Islam tentang dosa-dosa besar dan akibat-akibatnya. Aksesibilitasnya menjadikannya salah satu teks klasik yang paling langsung berguna bagi khalayak Muslim umum. Permintaan yang terus berlanjut untuk Al-Kaba'ir selama berabad-abad dan berbagai budaya mencerminkan relevansi abadi dari kepedulian intinya: membantu umat Islam mengidentifikasi pelanggaran-pelanggaran yang membawa akibat spiritual paling serius dan memotivasi mereka untuk menghindari pelanggaran-pelanggaran tersebut melalui kesadaran akan peringatan-peringatan al-Quran maupun penjelasan-penjelasan nabawi. Bahwa adz-Dzahabi mencapai ini dengan kehematan bahasa dan kesetiaan yang sedemikian besar kepada sumber-sumber primer tetap menjadi alasan utama mengapa karyanya telah melampaui banyak perlakuan yang lebih voluminus terhadap subjek yang sama.
content_en:
Al-Kaba'ir by adh-Dhahabi has been one of the most consistently used Islamic ethical texts since its composition, valued across all four major Sunni schools for its combination of hadith authenticity and practical ethical focus. Adh-Dhahabi was himself a Shafi'i in fiqh, but his status as a hadith master meant that scholars of all schools respected his hadith-based judgments.

The work's reception was particularly strong in educational settings where the goal was practical Islamic formation rather than advanced scholarly analysis. Its conciseness made it manageable for a general audience; its hadith grounding gave it scholarly credibility; and its focus on the most serious transgressions addressed a practical need that more comprehensive ethical works address only as part of a larger system. For a preacher delivering a sermon on avoiding sin, or a teacher introducing students to the concept of major transgressions in Islamic law and ethics, Al-Kaba'ir provided the most direct and authoritative reference.

Several other scholars wrote works in the same genre, including Ibn Hajar al-Haytami al-Makki's Az-Zawajir an Iqtiraf al-Kaba'ir (Deterrents from Committing Major Sins), which is more extensive and analytically detailed than adh-Dhahabi's work. The comparison between the two is instructive: adh-Dhahabi's conciseness makes it more accessible and emotionally immediate, while Ibn Hajar's comprehensiveness makes it more useful as a legal and scholarly reference. Both have been widely used and continue to be consulted.

In the contemporary Muslim world, Al-Kaba'ir has been published in numerous editions with varying degrees of annotation and is available in translation in many languages. It is commonly used in Islamic weekend schools, study circles, and online educational programs as an introduction to the Islamic understanding of major sins and their consequences. Its accessibility makes it one of the more immediately useful classical texts for a general Muslim audience. The continued demand for Al-Kaba'ir across centuries and cultures reflects the enduring relevance of its core concern: helping Muslims identify the transgressions that carry the most serious spiritual consequences and motivating them to avoid those transgressions through awareness of both the Quranic warnings and the prophetic explanations. That adh-Dhahabi accomplished this with such economy of language and such fidelity to the primary sources remains the chief reason his work has outlasted many more voluminous treatments of the same subject.

Adh-Dhahabi was himself a الشافعي in الفقه, but his status as a الحديث master meant that العلماء of all schools respected his الحديث-based judgments. The work's reception was particularly strong in educational settings where the goal was practical Islamic formation rather than advanced العلمي analysis. Its conciseness made it manageable for a general audience; its الحديث grounding gave it العلمي credibility; and its focus on the most serious transgressions addressed a practical need that more comprehensive ethical works address only as part of a larger system. For a preacher delivering a sermon on avoiding الذنب, or a teacher introducing students to the concept of major transgressions in Islamic law and ethics, Al-Kaba'ir provided the most direct and المعتمد reference. Several other العلماء wrote works in the same genre, بما في ذلك Ibn Hajar al-Haytami al-Makki's Az-Zawajir an Iqtiraf al-Kaba'ir (Deterrents from Committing Major الذنوب), which is more extensive and analytically detailed than adh-Dhahabi's work. The comparison between the two is instructive: adh-Dhahabi's conciseness makes it more accessible and emotionally immediate, بينما Ibn Hajar's comprehensiveness makes it more useful as a legal and العلمي reference. Both have been widely used and continue to be consulted. In the contemporary مسلم الدنيا, Al-Kaba'ir has been published in numerous editions with varying degrees of annotation and is available in translation in many languages. It is commonly used in Islamic weekend schools, study circles, and online educational programs as an introduction to the Islamic الفهم of major الذنوب and their consequences. Its accessibility makes it one of the more immediately useful الكلاسيكي texts for a general مسلم audience. The continued demand for Al-Kaba'ir across centuries and cultures reflects the enduring relevance of its الأساسي concern: helping المسلمون identify the transgressions that carry the most serious spiritual consequences and motivating them to avoid those transgressions through awareness of both the القرآني warnings and the prophetic explanations. That adh-Dhahabi accomplished this with such economy of language and such fidelity to the الأساسي sources remains the chief العقل his work has outlasted many more voluminous treatments of the same subject.
